title: Chevron Makes Plans for $5B Blue Hydrogen and Ammonia Project in Texas
content: Chevron plans to develop a $5-billion blue ammonia and hydrogen facility in Port Arthur, Texas, designed to produce lower-carbon hydrogen and ammonia through steam-methane reforming paired with carbon capture. The plant is set to break ground in 2027 and begin commercial operation by 2032 as part of the state's federally backed HyVelocity hydrogen hub. Called Project Labrador, the start date for the facility has become a critical element following the passage of the federal budget reconciliation package and its enactment on July 4.
author: Analysts Account
